title: alangium salviifolium var. hexapetalum
intro: Alangium salviifolium var. hexapetalum, also known as the six-petaled alangium, is a plant species in the Cornaceae family, characterized by its distinctive six-petaled flowers and leaves resembling those of the sage plant. Traditionally, Alangium salviifolium var. hexapetalum has been used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antidote for poison. In herbal preparations, Alangium salviifolium var. hexapetalum can be consumed as a decoction, made by boiling the roots and leaves in water, or used in combination with other herbs to create a syrup or tincture for various health purposes.
